1736600361673.text:
Here's something that I've learned recently from my mission. Something that I think I'll use for the rest of my life. 

It goes perfectly with this quote from a conference talk that I love. I think it portrays very simply what it means to succeed. It can be so easy to get down on ourselves when we're not perfect. But ultimately, the only "perfect performance" that's ever been seen on this earth was Jesus Christ. I've realized recently that focusing solely on RISING AGAIN, no matter how long it takes to get back up, can help me view my spiritual progress in a more "celestial" manner. The Lord delights in plainness, and all he wants is our effort. And with His help, we can "get up one more time than we go down, and win."

1736600388222.text: I'll try to get something to you soon as well ð

1736656468304.text:
My missionary experience has been great, and honestly feels like it has just begun! Which is weird because there are already so many things that have happened! So far on my mission I haven't had any Baptism's, but because I'm not using that as a measurement of success I don't think it's really weighing on my conscience. I know that what I have done so far has genuinely brought people to the Savior. It's already a win! That is no reason to get lazy though. Lately its felt hard to find time to go and seek out new people. I am hoping that this can be totally changed around! It's definitely a bit nerve-racking trying to share a gospel message in Japanese, but it's the best feeling ever when you've just walked away from doing it. I want to get to the point where I'm not even a little stressed about that. If I could give any advice to my "ikemen" (good looking) home ward it would be to first recognize the good you are already doing, (you are all here at church!!) and strive to find new and creative ways to serve those around you. I'll leave it up to you to decide, but I promise that you will have the energy of soul and mind to move forward as you see the light you are shining, and seek to strengthen it!
Love you guys!!

1753668181350.text:
But seriously, every time I read his journal, Iâm amazed by how full it is of loveâfor your family, your friends, for Godâand how grateful you are  for everything that happens.
 It always warms my heart and really inspires me.
 It reminds me that I need to be more grateful for all the things happening in my life too.
Lately, thereâs someone I work with that I really struggle to get along with,
 and itâs been hard for me to work the same shifts as them.
 But reading Elder Kilianâs writing made me realize that maybe this is a trial God has given me,
 and Iâve been trying to see it in a more positive light.
 So thank you for helping me shift my perspective.

1753680028477.text: It's maybe better that way lol

1754259725813.text:
even though Iâm not a church member yet, I feel so supported knowing that God is always watching over me.
 Just thinking that God loves me gives me such strength.
 Itâs like all those feelings of inferiority, or thinking that my parents love my siblings more than they love meâit all just stops mattering.
 And now, I donât feel scared about things the way I used toâthings like getting closer to others, being my authentic self, or stepping outside of my comfort zone.
 Iâve been able to do things that the old me would never have had the courage to do.
 So I really believe that there was a reason I met the missionaries.
 Thanks to all of them, I am who I am today, and Iâm deeply grateful for that.

1757163169157.text: Please keep Sakurako in your prayers. She said she's distancing herself from religion and anyone related to it. She said that I don't have anything to do with it, and she says we're still friends, but she said not to reply to or contact her. It has kind of felt like the end of the world these past few days, I feel so sick thinking about it. I realize that in the grand scheme of things she isn't necessarily a lost soul, but it feels that way to me right now. I don't care if we end up together in the end, just that she can come home with us at that last day. There might not be much I can do now, besides doing my best to be a missionary. My companion said maybe some day Sakurako will see that even through this hard time I continued pressing forward, and that I didn't give up. Maybe that would help her see just how much the Gospel means. I feel like it is partly gonna be my mission to bring her back, foolish as it sounds. I know that might not be the case but that's what my masculine creature inside of me is screaming lol. I feel like I'm in a dramatic novel now. I guess time will tell, but anyways, please keep her in your prayers. I will be fasting for her. Also if you can please keep me in your prayers too. I know you will, but I really need it right now. Any second I spend not finding or talking with members or playing Uno is painful. I'm sure it'll pass with time, but I hate this feeling. Love you guys, hope this isn't too concerning of a message, I'm still alive. I just need a little comfort right now.

1745847052522.text:
This is Sister Whites email lol
You can just feel her energy through it ð

çãããããã«ã¡ã¯ï¼(Hello everyone!) This week was packed full of so much FUN!!! â¨ï¸ðâ¨ï¸ I never want to leave Fukuoka!!! My muscles are still aching from my week of adventures! I'm so out of shape. ð I need to work on that, or this summer is going to be BRUTAL! ð¥µ Anyways, ä»ããã¬ãã¥ã¼ãã¾ãããï¼(Let's review now!)

Last Monday for P-Day, me and Maheå§å¦¹ went to Canal City which is a HUGE MALL!!! ðBut because we have no money, it was more like window shopping. ðªð Maheå§å¦¹ spent all of her money on a photo booth to take pictures with her favorite K-Pop artist. ð° That girl would die for K-Pop. ð I also LOVE Japanese arcade games!!! We played this drum game ð¥ and it hurt to move my arms afterwards. I have so much respect for percussionists. Those anime songs we played with were SO FAST!!! ð° We eventually wandered our way around the whole mall, and found a MINECRAFT POPUP STORE!!! ð¤¯ SAY LESS! I ZOOMED into that store like a baby zombie on a chicken (CHICKEN JOCKEY ðð§ââï¸)!!! ð As a child, I yearned for the mines. ð«¡âï¸ So OF COURSE I had to go in!!! And it was GLORIOUS!!! â¨ï¸ðâ¨ï¸ They had netherite swords, torches, axolotyl hats, anything you could ever imagine. I LOVE MINECRAFT!!! âï¸ð So that was indeed the highlight of my day. And the more I talk about this, the more I realize how nerdy I am. ð¤ð Thank you to everyone for accepting me for who I am. ð Love Y'all! ð¤  

We biked through more rain this week. ð§ I told Maheå§å¦¹ I wish I could be a passerby and watch myself bike, because I know my face looks like I'm in pain. ð But we found a cool shrine and took pictures next to it! I love living here. ð«¶

We were SO BUSY on Wednesday!!! ð We met up with a girl I found on Facebook! She is from Sri Lanka ð±ð° and is here as a college student! And she brought us chocolate mochi!!!! ð« SO KIND!!! We also had an appointment with an inactive family. I really want to teach the daughter and help her get baptized, but it's been hard to make appointments with them lately. But Wednesday was good!!! ð We were able to go and teach about the Atonement. And then we had to zoom our way over to the church for English class. ðºð¸ð¦ We had a lot of opportunities to meet with people that day, so I'm thankful. ð

Every Thursday we have weekly planning, and I like to go to new cafes and try their hot chocolates. ðð« So last Thursday, the one we went to also sold BAGELS!!! ð¥¯ THEY WERE SO GOOD!!! So now I need to go back and try all of the flavors!!! I got the cheese and salami bagel, and Maheå§å¦¹ got the chocolate one. SO YUMMY!!! I will definitely be back!!! 

Saturday was another busy day! We had activities all day! Last transfer, we could never go to activities because we never had any friends going. ð¢ But we went to EVERYTHING last week! And guess who took more pictures with the Giant Robot??? ð¤ IT WAS ME!!! We had to go to Lala Port to play Futsal, which is just like soccer except on a smaller field. It was SO FUN!!! It honestly made me miss playing! â½ï¸ And I wasn't half bad! I had a few good saves that I was proud of. ð But after that we had to bike 30 minutes back for dance class. ðand I am not a dancer. ð And then we ended the day off at Mcdonalds. ðð Japan Mcdonalds has this exclusive bacon and potato pie right now, and it's SO GOOD!!! ð¥ð¥ Now they just have to bring over the Flint and Meal and then I'll be happy. 

We had a friend come to church on Sunday!!! Her name is Momiji and she plays the flute! ðª She is a professional! Graduated from college with a music degree and everything. ð¤© She is friends with one of our new members, Tsukasaåå¼. We OBVIOUSLY connected because we both love music! ð¶ð¤­ And she thinks I'm funny, so that's a win win. ð I invited her to institute on Friday, and she wants to come!!! If she likes institute, I'm going to invite her to take gospel lessons. So everyone please pray that I can do that, because I'm lowkey freaking out. I hate rejection. Even though I get it every day. ð But it's different when the person is showing interest and then rejects you after. It stings a little more. But I can do this. She's awesome and because I care about her as a child of God that's all that matters. ð

Side note, do I look like a water hose drinker??? ð Greepé·è says the water tastes weird, but me and Maheå§å¦¹ disagreed. So then he goes, "Yeah, you look like you drink from a water hose." IT TASTES GOOD! As a kid that was the best water I ever drank on hot summer days. ð I'd like to hear others thoughts. Please let me know lol.

The Japanese word of the week is koibana (æã°ãª). It means Girl Talk, or Gossip! ð¤­ 

Scripture of the week: April 1980 General Conferece, N. Eldon Tanner, Celestial Marriages and Eternal Familes: "The happy home is one where the family lives together, works together, plays together, and prays together; where the parents show love and courtesy and demonstrate it to each other."

Thank you for reading! I hope this email was more exciting than last week's. Love you guys and have a great week!!! Always remember, God is good! ð«¶

Sister Karson White

1745848663870.text: All 80 gigs of our data have been used up by my comp (concerned laughing) so my email likely won't go through for a while, maybe not until I get wifi

1745849803387.text:
Tuesday
Today we were able to meet up again with Setsuko! We showed up at the park to talk and play Ground Golf and she showed up with a fresh haircut. She cuts her hair with scissors herself using two mirrors to see the back, shes awesome. She said we were both cute with our haircuts too. Before we played she recalled some of her memories she had of her two younger and very naughty brothers. When they were kids, their mom got a phone call from the school that the sons went too, and apparently one of them had broken all the windows Of the school ðð one of them also sprayed a fire extinguisher Off of the top floor. That's a whole new level of rambunctious. The funniest story she told us (in her opinion) was when one of her brothers was 4 years old, he jumped straight into a pond trying to catch a butterfly with his net. Setsuko acted this out with a big jump which definitely surprised me considering she's 75 (I think). She also has arthritis so I don't really know how she's moving around so well, she's super energetic and I'm all here for it. We also got to exchange Line with her, so now we have a solid contact method!! We didn't get to talk much about gospel stuff but It was a good chance to get to know her and her background better. Afterwards we did Some housing and I used the flower method ð¸ð»ð¹ðµð·ð¥ð¼ðºð ð Basically we ting the doorbell at the house of someone with a lot of pretty flowers or something and I'll ask if it's OK for me to take a picture of them. Today we tried it and got to talk with some lady in her front yard for a solid 5-10 minutes! She showed us all her different flowers and their names, and we even talked a bit about our missions. We invited her to Eikaiwa and it seems like she had a little bit of interest, she's practiced English by listening to the radio before! Hopefully we get to meet her again!

We visited art store lady again! I need to get her name memorized, I heard it once but totally forgot. She was pretty busy helping a guy get some art stuff for a news broadcast (crazy) but she saw we had an interest in calligraphy and tried to help us get set up with some free classes with some random teacher. I also picked out some watercolor paper to try, and when she saw me trying to look for more coins to pay for it, she grabbed my hand and took only the money I had (it was less than the paper) and sent me out of the store with my paper ð­ð shes so sweet but I literally want to like support the store. We will definitely visit again! We had Eikaiwa today, and before it started 2 random people (I think past students from like years ago) showed Up and wanted to say hi to us for a little while. We talked a bit about church stuff and a bit about Eikaiwa but it was super random. The two were maybe a couple (not sure) but they were for certain previously dance partners ððºThey were in their 70âs and weren't able to stay for long but it was nice to meet them. We played ping pong before Eikaiwa with one lady and she and I went back and forth for like 100 pings and tongs without missing a hit. It was like the spirit of ping pong was so strong ð» Eikaiwa was fun, afterwards Shiko showed us his acoustic-electric guitar. It was super cool, and it blows my mind just how light they are compared to electric guitars lol. Today Setsuko also sent us a bunch of voice memos which are so cute and always in English. She told one of her friends about me and Magbagoé·è and she wants to meet with us! From what Setsuko said she doesn't have much Of a gospel interest maybe, but Setsuko would like to talk about our beliefs all together with her. This will happen in 2 weeks, let's go!

Today we had exchanges with the Gobo elders, and I can say with surety that Gobo is the most Inaka (countryside) part of the mission. There's hardly anyone there. I was partnered up with Downsé·è and like 10 Percent of the doors we knocked even answered. One guy answered in a towel and had just gotten out of the bath. There was no reason he had to answer the door like that but it was awesome, props to him. Me and Matsuhisaé·è also played some PokÃ©mon that night, and the game was like 2 hours long ð± I ended up losing but I also chose not to just kill him in the first couple rounds so I feel validated. He also gave me much praise for my Japanese speaking, as a native he thinks I'm like cracked at Japanese. It's a very sweet sentiment, especially from another missionary who has likely seen all kinds of other missionaries and their own levels of Japanese. God has really blessed Me with an increased ability to speak and listen in Japanese as I have tried to align my will to His.

Today we had 3 Jimoty pickups, and because my comp don't got money ATM we biked to meet up with the 3 people. One of them was a bunch of assorted tools for 200Â¥, one of them was a couple guitar pickups for 1000Â¥, and another was a pair of 4kilogram exercise weights. Can you guess which one we picked up first?? The weights ð Can you guess How far we biked today? 30 Miles ððð The whole time I carried around those weights and it was wild. Not sure I've biked a longer distance in one day In my whole life. Also having the weights made it significantly tougher. We crossed 4 cities in between meeting with all of those people. As usual we invited them all to our Eikaiwa classes and I left them positive reviews on Jimoty. It was crazy for real. I also got real crisped up From the sun, I will attach a picture. While biking home we tried to visit a house Iâd been attempting to knock at least twice before. This time they answered the doorbell but immediately said no thanks even while I was saying how I loved their disney themed fence. Tragic. We also tried to say hi to a member by the church and she answered but we didn't exactly bring anything for her and she didn't have time for a longer visit so we gave a bit of an awkward greeting and left lol.

Sunday Surprise
At church we had a recently baptized member named Daniel show up to Sacrament meeting. He's been living in Japan for around 8 months and he's from Paraguay. We are hopefully gonna hang out with him on Pday! He lives pretty far and travels by bike so he hasn't gotten to attend church before now Since coming to Japan. Whatâs crazy is that he was tought and invited to baptism by a Sister Killian ð® Here's something crazy. A day before we met Daniel, a sister missionary named Dossettå§å¦¹ â¥ï¸ sent me a Facebook profile for an Elder Kade Killian who was called to serve in Japan 3 ish years ago. We met Daniel and it was Elder Kade Killians sister who served in Paraguay and taught Daniel about the gospel. Insane divine inspiration from Dossettå§å¦¹. Today me and Magbago played one of the card games he like called Union Arena And it was pretty fun. 

Random Missed note from last week, during exchanges met a high-school Girl on her porch and talked a bunch about Religion and what eternal life sounded like to her, and she even said that she imagines it would be living forever married ð 

Pday was miserably rainy, weren't able to hang out with Daniel cause of it. we did bike a little ways to get groceries though. I bought a foot pedal thingy for my guitar and another CD. Not at the grocery store if that wasn't apparent Lol. We have plans to meet with Jiro tonight, I'll have to talk about how it goes next week ð (it went well, I'm writing this late and after we met with him. We Invited him to meet up with the missionaries in Australia And he seems like he's excited to!)

Spiritual thought
This week has been a week of huge progress as a companionship. Getting out and doing Missionary work feels so good. Things aren't perfect, and there's definitely times where we aren't using our Time 100 percent perfectly but it's way better than it has been. I can promise to each of you reading this that no matter what difficulties you are facing, God knows your trials and struggles. I have really tried to see things from his perspective recently, Maybe holding my tongue against my companion or waking him up if I don't want to ð (not happening all that often Fortunately) or following a prompting from the spirit. Things really seem much better as you are striving to Follow God's plan. It's different in each of our lives, so I'd invite you to pray and sincerely ask God what His plan looks like for you. Maybe even ask what you can do. I'm sure you'll be surprised by the response you get, I definitely have been.

I love each and every one of you! I'm grateful to be serving the Lord. I hope that my email can be a light to some one of my friends out there ð

Elder Killian
